about.title: Frontend engineer building thoughtful interfaces.
about.subtitle1: 5+ years across web and mobile products. Currently shipping at
bullets: I've spent the last 5+ years working mostly in <1>JavaScript</1> and <2>React.js</2>, building web and mobile interfaces across startups and product companies.
At <1>Auzmor</1>, I work on the LMS platform as a Senior Software Engineer, across the web app and the <2>React Native</2> mobile app.
I studied Electronics and Communication at <1>Tezpur University</1>, graduating in 2020. Turned out writing code was more fun than building circuits.
I like knowing how things work under the hood. Performance, accessibility, and clean APIs are things I think about more than I probably should.
Outside work, I go to the gym 4 times a week, follow <1>cricket</1> way too closely, and try to <2>travel</2> whenever I get the chance.

work.title: Selected products, experiments, and shipped interfaces.
work.subtitle: A mix of product work and personal builds across the web, with a frontend-first lens on usability, polish, and speed.

blogs.title: Notes on frontend engineering and building for the web.
blogs.subtitle: Writing about React, accessibility, performance, and the smaller implementation details that shape good product experiences.

0.name: Don't Let the Tab Close Before Your Data Gets Out
0.description: Sending data to the Server when the User navigates away, closes/reloads the tab.

1.name: Lazy Loading in React Isn't Magic: Here's What Nobody Warns You About
1.description: Explore the challenges of lazy loading and code splitting in React, and discover strategies to overcome them for optimized performance and user experience.

2.name: Keyboard Users Exist. Here's How I Stopped Ignoring Them in Popups.
2.description: Learn how to improve the accessibility of your React application by ensuring that focus shifts seamlessly from triggering elements to popups. Explore a step-by-step guide utilizing React hooks and Semantic UI components to create a more inclusive user experience for keyboard and screen reader users.

footer.contactText: Whether you have a question or just want to say hi, my inbox is always open. I'll try my best to get back to you!
